defaults.application: Next.js App Router on Cloudflare Workers through @opennextjs/cloudflare
defaults.framework_decision: Compare Next.js, React Router framework mode, and Astro against the actual route, rendering, ecosystem, and Workers requirements; prefer Next.js App Router through OpenNext when it wins and record the adapter compatibility checks
defaults.ui: shadcn/ui with Tailwind CSS and semantic CSS tokens, selecting only required components, owning the generated source, and authoring product-specific composition
defaults.design_system: One selected design thesis, semantic brand tokens, responsive content rules, WCAG AA accessibility gates, and a governed signature move before page design
defaults.authentication: Clerk @clerk/nextjs with server-side authorization, production domain configuration, webhook verification, and CSP
defaults.payments: Stripe Checkout or Billing with raw-body signature verification, idempotent event handling, and fast 2xx acknowledgement
defaults.ai_gateway: Cloudflare AI Gateway for provider routing, logs, rate limits, caching where semantically safe, spend limits, and key references through Secrets Store
defaults.image_generation: OpenAI GPT Image 2 through the Image API, model gpt-image-2
defaults.database: Cloudflare D1 only when relational persistence is required
defaults.objects: Cloudflare R2 for user or generated binary assets and Next.js cache when applicable
defaults.asset_delivery: Bundle immutable brand assets with the Worker deployment; use R2 for generated, user-owned, or mutable assets; content-hash, optimize, and cache by lifecycle
defaults.coordination: Durable Objects only for stateful coordination that requires a single authoritative instance
defaults.asynchronous_work: Queues for decoupled delivery; Cloudflare Workflows for durable multi-step execution, retries, waits, or human approval

decision_rules: Use no Cloudflare product merely because it exists; justify each binding from a concrete workload.
Cloudflare-first is a preference, not a truth claim: compare viable framework paths and document any exception required by workload or verified compatibility.
Reverify the current Next.js/OpenNext, React Router, Astro, auth, billing, and binding support before implementation; do not infer compatibility from package names.
Own semantic tokens and component source; do not mistake a component library for a design direction or ship its demo composition unchanged.
Keep authn, authorization, billing entitlement, and application roles as separate decisions.
Treat Stripe and Clerk webhooks as untrusted, verify signatures, deduplicate events, and record side-effect receipts.
Route model traffic through AI Gateway unless a documented capability gap requires a direct provider call.
Never cache personalized or safety-sensitive model output without an explicit cache-key and privacy analysis.
Generated imagery requires provenance, moderation, alt text, storage lifecycle, and a non-generated fallback plan.
Every planned asset needs a stable ID, exact placements, production method, output variants, rights/provenance, accessibility behavior, and acceptance checks.
Prefer one Workers application until scale, isolation, or independent deployment creates a measured reason to split it.
